steps.title: Fastest path: use the triangle sum
steps.content: Use the fact that the three interior angles of a triangle add to $180^\circ$. Once the sum of two angles is known, subtract that sum from $180^\circ$.

steps.title: Apply the angle sum property of triangles
steps.content: In any triangle, the sum of the interior angles is $180^{\circ}$. For triangle HJK: $$\angle H + \angle J + \angle K = 180^{\circ}$$ Given that $\angle H + \angle J = 42^{\circ}$, we substitute: $$42^{\circ} + \angle K = 180^{\circ}$$ $$\angle K = 180^{\circ} - 42^{\circ} = 138^{\circ}$$ D is correct.
